title: adapter.py: create_adapter accesses private Node attribute _on_receive
description:
File: python/src/lichen/interface/meshtastic/adapter.py
Lines: 414-424

The create_adapter function accesses a private attribute:

```python
original_callback = getattr(node, "_on_receive", None)

def on_receive(payload: bytes, sender: object) -> None:
    if original_callback:
        original_callback(payload, sender)
    adapter.on_message_received(payload, iid)

node.set_on_receive(on_receive)
```

This pattern has several issues:
1. Accesses _on_receive which is private (underscore prefix)
2. If Node changes its implementation, this breaks silently
3. The wrapper doesn't preserve the original callback's signature properly

Impact: Fragile coupling to Node implementation details. Changes to Node class could break Meshtastic adapter silently.

Fix: Either:
1. Node should expose a public way to add multiple callbacks (callback chain/list)
2. Or document that _on_receive is semi-public and part of the adapter contract
3. Or use a decorator pattern that doesn't rely on private attributes
